name: Le Cunard
description: An ode to the golden age of transatlantic travel, Le Cunard distils the romance of the ocean liner into pure Art Deco geometry — funnel, horizon and spray rendered with the confidence of 1930s Paris. An object of departure for any wall.

descriptions.aboutArtist: These editions descend from a folio of 1930s French advertising and ballet posters attributed to N. Birzinska, a name from the Russian émigré circles that animated the Paris of the années folles. Our founder first encountered the originals through a collector in St. Petersburg — a long winter afternoon, a pot of black tea, and a portfolio of brittle proofs that had not left the city in ninety years. Stasera’s editions are faithful restorations of that portfolio, printed to archival standard so a near-forgotten body of work can hang on the wall again.
descriptions.deliveryAndReturns: Produced to order and shipped from the closest available fulfilment partner where possible. Returns are accepted for damaged, faulty, or incorrect items in line with our returns policy.
descriptions.productDetails: Printed on premium archival paper with rich colour reproduction, a refined matte finish, and careful handling from production to dispatch.

name: Marouf
description: Drawn from the Orientalist reverie of the interwar Parisian stage, Marouf conjures the lavish fantasy of 1930s opera — gold, indigo and the slow theatre of the Belle Époque’s last breath.

name: Tyler, the Creator — Call Me If You Get Lost
description: In 2021 Tyler, the Creator released Call Me If You Get Lost — a passport-stamped travelogue of a record, Tyler Baudelaire in linen and chrome. This poster reduces that maximalism to a single graphic gesture: luxury, motion and an artist at the height of his powers.

descriptions.aboutArtist: Designed for Stasera by J. Kadera, part of the studio’s circle of contemporary graphic artists. Kadera works in a reductive idiom — distilling the iconography of modern music into type, colour and negative space; less illustration than typographic portraiture. Each piece is produced exclusively for Stasera.

name: Blade Runner 2049
description: Denis Villeneuve’s Blade Runner 2049 (2017) is science fiction as painting — Roger Deakins’ fogged oranges and brutalist greys. This poster distils that world to atmosphere: monolithic, melancholic, rain on neon.

The film series is produced exclusively for Stasera.
